channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: Why your body feels achy and uneven: 5 everyday habits that strain one side more than the other
item.description: Physiotherapists say daily habits like sitting and carrying bags can cause muscle imbalances. You don’t need to be an athlete to develop muscle imbalances. From the way you sit to how you carry your bag, physiotherapists explain how common daily habits can make one side of your body tighter or weaker – and what to do about it.

item.title: US Supreme Court reinstates Republican-favoured Texas electoral map
item.description: The US Supreme Court reinstated a Texas electoral map backed by President Donald Trump. The reinstated map, backed by President Donald Trump, could flip key districts to Republicans. US Supreme Court reinstates Republican-favoured Texas electoral map.

item.title: Box to launch Box Automate to speed up customers' business with AI, CEO says
item.description: Box will launch Box Automate to speed up business with AI. Box to launch Box Automate to speed up customers' business with AI, CEO says.

item.title: Carney to Launch Sovereign Wealth Fund to Distance Canada’s Economy From U.S.
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney announced a sovereign wealth fund for Canada. The sovereign wealth fund announced by Prime Minister Mark Carney will be far smaller than ones in other oil producers like Norway and the Middle East.

item.title: Afghan officials say Pakistani strikes killed 7, wounded 85 in first attacks since peace talks
item.description: Pakistani strikes killed 7 and wounded 85 in Afghanistan, according to Afghan officials. Pakistan's Ministry of Information and Broadcasting dismissed Afghan media reports and official statements about the strikes on the university as “a blatant lie”.

item.title: Losing S$150,000, stealing from work: Why this former gambling addict will not watch live World Cup
item.description: A former gambling addict lost S$150,000 and stole from work. He will not watch the live World Cup to avoid relapse. Counsellors and psychologists are bracing for a rise in gambling addiction cases with the FIFA World Cup kicking off in June.

item.title: Beyond the ‘iron rice bowl’: Why more young Chinese are choosing flexible work
item.description: One in three Chinese workers are in flexible jobs, with younger generations seeking autonomy. Around one in three workers in China are now in flexible jobs, led by a younger generation seeking greater autonomy despite the trade-offs.

item.title: Bahrain strips 69 people of citizenship over Iran support
item.description: Bahrain stripped 69 people of citizenship over alleged Iran support. Rights groups called it a blatant abuse of power. Rights groups have described the move as a 'blatant abuse of power'.

item.title: Melania Trump urges ABC to 'take stand' on Jimmy Kimmel after widow joke
item.description: Melania Trump urged ABC to take a stand against Jimmy Kimmel. He made a joke about her being a widow. In a parody aired days before the White House Correspondents' Dinner, Kimmel called Melania an "expectant widow".

item.title: Who holds the cards in Iran-US talks?
item.description: The US and Iran claim to have the upper hand in negotiations. Both sides assert control. Who holds the cards in Iran-US talks?

item.title: Iran FM in Russia says US has offered new round of talks
item.description: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi met in Russia to discuss a possible new round of talks with the US. Russia is hosting Iran’s Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi in St. Petersburg for talks on a possible new round of talks.

item.title: China seeks to block US tech giant Meta from AI acquisition
item.description: China is blocking US tech giant Meta from an AI acquisition amid tightened scrutiny. Bejing tightens scrutiny of AI industry amid intensifying geopolitical rivalry with the US over the technology.

item.title: Canada Says Its Gold Is Traceable and Clean. So We Traced It.
item.description: Canada claimed its gold is traceable and clean. An investigation ended at a Colombian mine controlled by a drug cartel. We ended up in a Colombian mine controlled by a drug cartel.

item.title: Iran Offers Plan to Focus on Strait of Hormuz and Delay Nuclear Talks
item.description: Iran proposed opening the Strait of Hormuz to shipping traffic and lifting the U. S. blockade in a new offer delivered on Sunday. The plan delays nuclear talks with the U. until later.

item.title: Canada’s New Route to Citizenship Has Thousands of Americans Lining Up to Apply
item.description: Canada's new citizenship route has thousands of Americans applying through ancestral ties. Canada has opened a route to citizenship for people who can prove they have a Canada-born ancestor. Millions could qualify, and Americans are already lining up to apply.

item.title: King Charles and Queen Camilla arrive for high-profile US state visit
item.description: King Charles and Queen Camilla arrived in the US for a state visit. The trip aims to strengthen ties amid tensions over the Iran conflict. The British monarch’s US visit underscores shared history amid strained ties and tensions over the Iran conflict.

item.title: Hezbollah and Israel swap threats and strikes across Lebanon’s border
item.description: Hezbollah and Israel exchanged threats and strikes across Lebanon's border, escalating tensions. Iran-linked group reiterates defiance; Israeli defence minister threatens to ‘burn all of Lebanon'. Hezbollah and Israel swap threats and strikes across Lebanon’s border.

item.title: Elon Musk trial against Sam Altman to reveal OpenAI power struggle
item.description: Elon Musk's trial against Sam Altman begins with jury selection on Monday, potentially impacting the balance of power in AI. The trial's outcome may influence control over OpenAI, a key player in the field. Musk and Altman are locked in a dispute over the organization's direction.

item.title: King Charles III arrives in U.S. on delicate mission to restore U.K.-U.S. relationship
item.description: King Charles III arrived in the US to restore the UK-US relationship. The President referred to him as a friend and great guy. The President has spoken in glowing terms about King Charles, repeatedly referring to the monarch as his “friend” and a “great guy”.

item.title: Trump Discusses Iran Offer To Open Straits Of Hormuz
item.description: Trump discussed Iran's proposal to open the Hormuz Strait with his national security team. US President Donald Trump on Monday met his national security team to discuss Iran's latest proposal to open the Hormuz Strait, the crucial waterway for transporting one-fifth of the global oil supplies.
